TITLE      Measurement of the 13C(alpha,n0)16O Differential       C3076001    3 
           Cross Section from 0.8 to 6.5 MeV                      C3076001    4

INSTITUTE  (1USANOT,1USAORL,1USAOHO,3HUNDEB,1USATEN,1USALAS)      C3076001   18

           Spokane, Washington 99258, USA                         C3076001   20 
FACILITY   (VDG,1USANOT) The University of Notre Dame Nuclear     C3076001   21 
           Science Laboratory 5 MV Stable ion Accelerator for     C3076001   22 
           Nuclear Astrophysics.                                  C3076001   23 
DETECTOR   (SCIN) The Oak Ridge National Laboratory Deuterated    C3076001   24 
           Spectroscopic Array (ODeSA). The array consisted of    C3076001   25 
           nine deuterated scintillators mounted on a swing arm,  C3076001   26 
           which was rotated to two different positions, with an  C3076001   27 
           offset of 7.5 degree, in order to map 18 point         C3076001   28 
           angular distributions between 0 and 157.5 degrees.     C3076001   29 
SAMPLE     (6-C-13,ENR=0.99) Enriched (99%) 13C powder was        C3076001   30 
           evaporated onto a 0.5 mm Ta backing, which also        C3076001   31 
           served as a beam stop, creating a thin layered         C3076001   32 
           reference target of 10.3(6) microg/cm2. The target     C3076001   33 
           thickness was determined by measuring the energy loss  C3076001   34 
           over the narrow resonance in the 13C(alpha,n)16O       C3076001   35 
           reaction at Ealpha = 1.05 MeV.                         C3076001   36 
ERR-ANALYS (DATA-ERR) The uncertainties on the data points are    C3076001   37 
           point-to-point, which are dominated by the unfolding   C3076001   38 
           uncertainty and target degradation corrections. An     C3076001   39 
           additional common mode systematic uncertainty of 13%   C3076001   40 
           is also present that is not included.                  C3076001   41 
           (ERR-SYS) Systematical uncertainty.                    C3076001   42 
           (ERR-1) Charge collection.                             C3076001   43 
           (ERR-2) Stopping power.                                C3076001   44 
           (ERR-3) Intrinsic efficiency.                          C3076001   45 
           (ERR-4) MCNP/Geometric efficiency.                     C3076001   46 
CORRECTION The most significant corrections to the present data   C3076001   47 
           came from neutron scattering on the target backing,    C3076001   48 
           holder, and other beam components very close to the    C3076001   49 
           target. These corrections were simulated using MCNP6,  C3076001   50 
           where a detailed model of the setup was created.       C3076001   51 
           These corrections were most significant at 0 and 90    C3076001   52 
           degrees, up to 30%, but were less than 10%, otherwise. C3076001   53
